A conversation is us being made to understand what we currently understand by the one source of all power and knowing and being. All truth is God's truth, and all falsehood is chaff to be cast into the fire, the friction that is eternal existence and extance.

I recommend looking at the parable of the sower.
https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew%2013&version=NIV

This is pretty meta. For most of the parables Jesus does not explain it, but the very one where he explains the parable is the one where the very parable is about the fact that people will not understand his parables. As in, they will not see how the sign relates to the significance.

If you do not see how the sign relates to the significance, it is because the spirit of truth does not bring you from the sign to the significance. Those who are brought from sign to significance yet reject this significance are what we might call Satanists. They are the ones who "blaspheme against the Holy Spirit".

A common thread throughout all your writings is you giving up and feeling like there is no point. For the Christian, they recognize that the point of anything is ultimately not up to us. We align to God when we respond to his will and love what he offers in every moment. Way gives way to way. Truth gives truth to truth. Life gives life to life.
There is a point, and there is a center. You are not that center, far from it. There is a central axis about which all shall turn forevermore.
Is there not something futile and death-seeking about not only the disposition to believe that there is no point to something, but also to say it aloud, like you are an evangelist of defeat?

for example he consistently frames israel as denying him being a statesman, and talks about the political pressures and difficulties he goes through

"This expansion works perfectly all the way to Yellow- but this is where the spiral gets wrong: after Yellow, development reverses direction. Instead of expanding further outward, the path turns inward. People at higher stages become simultaneously more and less.

At Yellow, we’ve built scaffolding sturdy enough to dismantle itself and come out wiser but lighter on the other side. The tools we’ve gathered - systems thinking, perspective-taking, complexity navigation, paradox resolution - aren’t for fixing the world or reprogramming others. They’re for turning inward to find the delusion in ourselves.

This bridges two traditions that have been talking past each other:

* Western psychology maps construction but stops at Yellow, declaring victory at peak ego-sophistication

* Eastern dharma maps deconstruction but assumes you’ve already built something stable to deconstruct

The diamond reveals they’re consecutive phases of one complete journey."

Right now I'm thinking about what a state is
Starting from "a state is a partition of observations"
well yeah, but that's the explanation behind, like, complex language, generically 😝
But an observation implies an observer. What's an observer? An observer is itself a boundary, across which information flows in and out.

A state is a partition is a boundary, an observer is a boundary. In the case of the observer, we ask what it's like to be the boundary itself. In the case of a state, we ask what the existence of a boundary looks like from the outside.

A given observer can perceive many states. There are not an infinite number of potential partitioning, but there might as well be, the number of possible states grows super-exponentially with the number of observations.
For a finite observer, this means that not all states that can be perceived will be. The observer can only perceive the subset of states that they consider.
Which states should those be? They should be the most useful ones. What's useful to an observer? Roughly, predictive accuracy over future observations conditioned on measurements (actions).

What makes a state useful? Well, it's not clear that there is a closed form answer to that. But generally speaking, states that occur regularly are more useful than ones that occur rarely or never. States whose presence predicts other states are more valuable. States whose presence predicts change to other states are more valuable. States whose presence predicts change to other states conditioned on actions are more valuable. States whose presence predicts other states which are more common are more valuable than those who predict other states which are rare.
There is probably an infinite regress of such contingencies, every way that a state could turn out to improve predictive accuracy.

https://x.com/taijitu_sees/status/1974113001163870607?t=858Krp5G59H8IliU_zzS5w&s=19

Rationalists have a blindspot to narratives, but are still very much affected by them. Most if not all our brain software comes from acausal narratives.

to me its clear that if certain concepts appear together frequently you have enough material to build models

to a rationalist this is basically delusional

Yeah, it's kinda wild. They tend to be very conservative in deciding where to draw lines and it limits how much they can see and work with. But they do get to see more details. And you can invert this for the mystics. 

Combine these as a algorithmic sweep and you just covered two major blindspots better than 90% of the public 😂

whats crazy is they DO make arbitrary categories where none exist

they just define it that way and even without proof

which is exactly what they think can't happen

Yeah, that's unavoidable. It's just their categories are microscopic and pedantic and they call it "axiomatic" or from "first principles" often failing to realize there's an infinite recursive chain back and forward with an often simple set of patterns that can explain it.
if i observe my awareness when arguing with a rationalist their framing shuts down a part of my brain that is curious and trying to build new knowledge
i truly think they aren't in creation mode
like if you are in creation mode you wonder and nothing is certain
and you make bold claims for fun
and you see whats the boldest claim you can make that will potentially survive criticism eventually
thats like the basic instinct
i guess stories is where the sense of wonder is
like "just imagine"
if the tales about einstein are true then that guy was in creation mode, he proclaimed the importance of imagination
maybe there is a reason why science has stagnated in so many domains

like how it should work is, when i encounter someone else doing what im doing:

- i notice this is highly speculative territory
- i switch to creation mode
- i accept that this endeavor is fundamentally possible, even if the chance is low of it working out
- i bring up ideas and framings and concerns of my own that help explore the territory
- we see where there is concordance and discordance

how it works with (usually) rationalists is:

- there is no check for whether its speculative or not
- we just amplify any beliefs we have that disqualify the endeavor (even if in normal situations these beliefs themselves are speculative)
- unless it agrees with our beliefs already
creation mode is kinda like steelmanning but with the intent of helping develop the framework instead of rephrasing the framework in charitable terms

these modes can be codified and labeled imo

i guess its kinda like the philo sophia and philo nikea thing perhaps
Funny how there're two failure modes for curiosity. Understanding a part well is the whole and seeing the whole is understanding all the parts well.

The rationalists work as turbo literalists and collapse things too aggressively to create closed systems. Lots of black box abstractions without realizing the guts of the box could be filled with multiple different solutions.
It's also funny, both of these failure modes are to restrict chaos and new information from getting in too quickly.
So, I think there's probably two different strategies/interfaces to get the information in on each mode. But I'm unsure how to describe the process of each.

Perhaps the root is the way of pointing out how categories break down on each side of this bridge. Using set theory to expand or contract categories just enough for them to take in without triggering perspective shock making the ego feel threatened.
what are the failure modes?
The uniting factor is thinking they have all the information needed. But the difference is having a wide focus or narrow focus.
Rationalists have narrow focus.

when I read about GAN I felt like it's so obviously the way our brain does it, a descriminator that has to detect fake signals and the generator (or whatever they are called) that has to fool the discriminator

even the failure modes are so real, like if the descriminator is too good the generator stops generating entirely and training collapses and if the generator is too good the system fails to generalize

it's famously unstable and balance is needed for good training 

I am still salty that GAN isn't AGI
Yeah, it's taking advantage of the generative void which really astounds me. People do this every second, but don't often take advantage of the emptyness that generates novelty.

(there are interesting pathologies

like if the descriminator is too strong it becomes overly confident so the generator can't learn, because everything it generates is wrong with 100% certainty, so there is no "weaker" signals that say "likely wrong but there is a chance" so that it can learn from that )
Reminds me of "face blindness" where the face detector in the brain is broken and every face looks exactly the same to them. Wild how the boundaries work there.
Like, one side is there are no faces because you can't put them into a category while the other side is everything is a face and both effectively are blind. 😂
Ok, now I dropped the video. I'm setting an expected value of $1/view of value created from simply a story.

it's not infinite -- there are only a finite number of possible partitionings of a given amount of data, and observers always have a finite amount of data unless it's an infinite observer, but those don't exist -- no boundary lasts forever. the observer that is "the whole of reality" could be seen to exist forever, but that one has no boundary (since nothing is outside of it)

it's not about number of steps, it's about matching distributions. in the discrete formulation, at every timestep you have some observations, and those observations get partitioned by all of your states.

it's not a problem that there could be an infinite regress here. it's like saying there is an infinite potential planning horizon...you could always plan for one state longer. for any finite being, eventually you stop. ditto here, eventually you stop considering various cross-contingencies and move forward.

ruliad & boundaries

i read up on the ruliad view and it imports far too many assumptions for my taste. The desire to avoid infinite regress by assuming terminal frames, for example, is something that I deeply want to avoid.
the FEP and predictive accuracy

Down the road,  you start having chained interpreters that produce a state which describes the new prepared codebook followed by the action bits, which is then interpreted by the outer interpreter by executing the action bits. The result of that execution outer interpreter are corresponding co-action bits, aka the observation state required to re-invoke the first interpreter.

once you have that, you have an ongoing object over time which is effectively an observer with a boundary exchanging information with the environment. this licenses use of the Free Energy Principle to take its internal states as a generative model which minimizes expected free energy.

and thus the memory states must encode expectations in the form of a prior distribution over observations, actions, and a coarse-grained dynamics model of the parts of the environment relevant for choosing actions and predicting observations.

those priors are a partitioning of the memory state of the interpreter, which is to say they are substates. Specifically, they're useful substates -- they predict observations conditioned on action, and action conditioned on operation
change in the priors is based on minimizing the expected predicted surprise (technically expected free energy, since that bounds surprise and you can't actually minimize surprise)
state-dependency calculation

This is really just the FEP again. The FEP says that the agent will learn a model that minimizes its expected free energy given its history, which is maxinimizing accuracy while minimizing complexity. Adding more cross-slicing contingencies increases complexity, and at some point no longer pays for itself with accuracy.

Not respecting human life tends to make you lose. But the proper respect for human life is to be modeled by God, not by our current views on peace and non-aggression. The biblical standard for proper respect for human life included genocide.
“If genocide has good results in the eternal sense, it cannot be wrong.” This statement might bother people, but it necessarily must hold.

Oh, I realized that I did not address the core issue here. 

In the Christian ontology of winning, it is impossible to win without truth or love, because winning, truth, and love are one thing. They cannot be divided. They can only be distinguished in the sense that human concepts point to smaller things. It happens to be that "God is victory" is less commonly stated than "God is life", "God is love", "God is truth", etc.

The rank ordering between human concepts of victory, truth, and love is necessarily something that can only happen in light of sin, because teleologically these three are exactly the same thing (what is ultimately winning also is what is perfectly good and so also constitutes love, which actually means that genocide in the Old Testament is loving) so any separation between the three is an indication of our own disorder.

Almost every single time I talk about God being the standard for victory, “victory” could be replaced by “love” or “truth” because they actually are the same thing. The “victory” part is just less intuitive to those who are not that fluent in this way of thinking, versus the idea of God as just a big feel-good idea rather than as a necessary existence.

The idea of God as being an arbitrary judge of morality without sense is much more popular in mainstream culture and thought than the correct view of God being the very source of morality. Only because there actually is winning and losing (existence and non-existence, or life and death) and the Christian warrant to win (“Christians should win” vs. “Rationalists should win”) do we have such a thing as morality in the Christian worldview.
Modern law courts are also a diagnostic with a cure. Prison terms *are* cures, just more on the collective scale.

I think that this is a false dichotomy. The forensic sense is coincident with the restorative sense. Man indeed does stand as a criminal, by disposition and by action. Man also stands as a patient, both from what was done to him and what he has done to himself and others. The restoration to health is coincident with the restoration to justice. Grace *is* a payment for the distance between a fallen state and a perfect state; medicine is a payment. By abstraction, this is just true.

I think that it is possible to overindex on the idea of forensic courts when it comes to speaking about the atonement of Christ on the cross, to the point where God no longer has the freedom to forgive sin however he wants. But it is just straight up wrong to marry the connotation of courts vs. hospitals in discussing this. The isomorphism between the two concepts cares not for the everyday differences.

Restoration to physical and mental health is analogous to restoration to justice, unless what we are talking about justice is completely arbitrary. (Christian morality is not arbitrary.) A state of Christian justice is also a state of health. There is no reasonable way to construct an ontology of justice where this does not hold.

Actual justice, rather than *systems* of justice, is directly about cause and effect, about will, about distance from the one and only way. God is not a system, and his justice is not merely the equivalent of a paper trading simulation. The sins of people, whether fallen upon them or actively committed by them, actively result in distance. The great physician is also the king upon whose shoulder rests the duty and privilege of vengeance. God’s vengeance is also God’s mercy. God’s vengeance on our behalf, the defeat of death, is also the grace of eternal life.
God obliterated the cancer that is death, and so long as one does not aspire to put their identity in that defeated cancer rather than in the healthy body, they are assuredly within eternal life.

TL;DR: The Eastern Orthodox fixation on a health model rather than a justice model fundamentally misunderstands justice and the accordance of justice systems, incl. courts, to its true referent in justice.

<@1250084478152478773> Only looking at the opening paragraphs, I would say this text is selecting mostly for people with a fairly naive theology, regardless of tradition. Every serious tradition connects morality to what we associate with "physicality", and answers the question of why there is something rather than nothing (for Christianity it is ontological). Also, though I believe in fine tuning, I do not think it is that evident why one would adopt this frame over the idea of incidental order from arbitrary conditions. (Christianity solves this by saying that God has the ultimate freedom and is the very source of all goodness and thus rightness, so *anything* God does is perfect and thus perfectly tuned for it is the very standard for measuring tuning.)

Also, I wonder about how much you get into the cybernetic nature of the observer being situated within the very reality they are observing. When I look at my hand, what I see and what the things I see mean to me and what I do about the meaning I get are all things that themselves are dependent on the outside. I usually would say that an observer-centric model just defaults to solipsism or gnosticism unless it explicitly tackles the way in which observations of creatures are contingent.

Based on the first page alone I would definitely not recommend this text to anyone who is not that knowledgeable about classical theology just because it normalizes so many ideas which I consider incorrect. Like, classical theism has always been able to see all of reality as a sort of multidimensional (beyond three dimensions spatial dimensions and one temporal dimension) sculpture, and speaking about process within the context of the logic of the one completed creation. 

There is also a lot of idea dropping of individual ideas without that much explanation. I think ORI people would understand most of it, or is high enough agency to look it all up, but this edges on schizo pop "white girl" science territory.

The separation of science and theology is presumed in the opening pages of this text, and I think that this is mostly wrong when we are talking about Western science, as it relies on the foundation that observations actually accord with reality or that observations are meaningfully correlated to one another. 

This paper might be more interested on just expanding the *scientific* paradigm to include things that are not immediately physical, but in some sense it has *always* done this. When science uses ontologies, it is necessarily employing something not physical, even if the referent for the ontology has physical properties. In the sense that science tries to assert that it actually describes reality, it is *already* theological.

On page 5 I question the use of "probable" over "likely", though this is a quibble relative to most people's ontology. Probability and likelihood are not the same; probability is a weighting within a model, and the likelihood is the probability of a model being the correct one in your meta-model.

that I did not spring whole cloth into this world

Like, I don't think people actually think "The most likely model is the one where God has above a 50% probability of existing"; I think people think "The most likely model is the one where God 100% exists." Aside, not a bone to pick with S. A. Senchal specifically, but it really annoys me how subjective and objective morality are framed. It seems to be that people think one is true at the expense of the other, when I think that they are concurrently true. There is objective morality in the sense that each subject has a will which they apply to reality, and so there is a landscape (heh, even Sam Harris likes the idea of a moral landscape) of outcomes, and for any set of objectives (most importantly the very survival of the subject within its substrate of identity) there are more and less instrumental actions. So in this view, conventional morality is a thing which factors into objective morality in the sense that the fact that people will throw you in a cell or oppress you for something like i.e. having homosexual relations necessarily factors into whether homosexual relations are sinful.

I actually got to God through the continued affirmation of *subjective* morality and the idea of an is-ought separation. This naturally led to a wireheading phase, and then a realization that no one can actually completely freely wirehead as their own experiences are contingent, and so there actually is a sort of objective landscape through which you can traverse with your utility function to capture more utility.

> naturalism reduces all mental states to physical states governed by natural laws, eliminating genuine reasoning

I really do not want to defend naturalism, but I also think that a sufficiently sophisticated and honest naturalism actually can make a form of genuine reasoning that makes logic (esp. in the classical logic sense) contingent on actual physical states and the correspondence between mental states and outcomes. In fact, this is closer to a *true* conception of divine logic than just something like the worship of syllogisms.
Also, I am online enough that when I read the rest of this section I found myself smirking and thinking, "Okay, Plato!"

I will give fewer comments on the atheistic arguments section because I think that it is not that useful to harp on them, but the ontology that I have of evil as nonexistence mostly just resolves many problem of evil (theodicy) conceptions. Anything that exists exists at the expense of its exact negation (X and not X cannot both be extant in the sane sense of X), so evil just exists as a natural consequence of good existing and there being any ability to discern anything except the good. (The fruit of knowledge of good and evil is not just hypothetical reasoning, but it is actually quite similar in terms of how at some point we started identifying in things that never happened, are not happening now, and will never happen.)

Whenever I read a long text I try to think about the ways in which I could summarize it into something much shorter, ideally into the length of a single Bible "chapter" or "verse". It actually feels like the parable of the talents (or the minas) expresses a reconciliation between atheistic and theistic conceptions really well, and ends up just dividing the discussion into whether you believe in God or Satan. 

In the parable of the talents, the ones who follow the ruler while he is away are rewarded in the future kingdom based on their performance of his instructions and will, with the better performers getting more and the worse performers getting less. Meanwhile, those who rejected the ruler are taken out the back and Old Yellered. This is actually a very strong affirmation of might makes right being the foundation of morality, and of reality itself being a might which no one can reject. All the discussion of theology when shifted to reality just results in a discussion where everyone agrees practically without resistance. (Many atheists are actually in effect theists, in the sense that they believe in morality and also in the correspondence of reality as seen with reality yet to be seen or eternally beyond, and also believe that we are in relationship with this reality, whether through science or through experience.)

In the sense that there are better or worse ways of attaining an object, those who accept the object at hand also implicitly recognize the oughts relative to that object. Those who see an object but then reject the object "blaspheme" that object. Hence why blasphemy of the Holy Spirit is the unforgivable sin.

Techno gnosticism is a term that sometimes floats around and I think it matches pretty well here. To a lot of the Landian crowd, reality just *is* spirit and just *is* runes and symbolic manipulation. Meanwhile, the tree of life pegs reality to extance and *not* our knowledge of good and evil.

I don't think this is that uncommon of a mistake when Kabbalah formulae intersects with someone's godless thinking. It just is treated as ABRACADABRA stuff and essentially becomes the edgier version of manifesting.

As I said some ostensibly "Christian Kabbalists" exist but they from what I see have completely different dispositions toward reality than the Kabbalah I see kicking around. Might not be completely fair to Kabbalah to tie it so much to its associations, but I do actually do the same for various Christian theologies incl. my own. The fruits of an idea do factor into my default disposition regarding spreading them versus suppressing them.

Esoteric studies among Christians is something which I see as a net negative to the faith. I read that Martin Luther had some brushes with some esoteric thinking that ended up entrenching some of his views early on and which still carries on in some Lutheran thought. (Compare the Lutheran rose to the Rosicrucian rose.)

In fact I think the best argument against Protestantism (as a Protestant) is just how wayward people can get when they start either going too much into occult thinking or reacting too much to occult thinking. (Reacting to something gives it power.) Both witches and witch burnings are kinda exacerbated with Protestants. Also, look at all the cults that came out of nineteenth century America. Something weird in the religious water then.

This is where I think institutional churches that help to peg sign to significance and give authoritative confessional standards for interpretation is essential if we want to have a church where the highest binding institutional authority is the scriptures.

The SD-map narrowing is more about direction shift rather than becoming "less.", or having to sacrifice something. It’s meant to point to practice of direct experience, to see more clear, rather than practice of building mental imagery and then (mistakenly/seeing less clear) treating that as a direct experience - this mental imagery (treated as direct experience) is often the root cause of much mental trouble and tangles that will remain until we eradicate the tangles with direct experience: 

If a person is depressed and the depression is rooted in delusion (for example: everyone hates me, I'm not good enough (with endless variations)),

I _think_ the reason I said "sacrifice" is because it feels that way before making the leap? Like if you think:

- "everyone hates me, I'm not good enough"
- "therefore, I need to get really good at project XYZ"
- "I am making progress at XYZ, I'm so close to solving all of my problems, and ALL the suffering will have been worth it"

It's kind of like digging yourself into a hole, shoveling furiously (but you're only getting deeper in), and at this point your problem being solved (getting out of the hole) feels like it requires you to sacrifice all the progress
(but, of course, once you see through it, you realize, you were never making progress. So it's not a "real" sacrifice)
i think this is important to understand because i think this is where people get stuck, giving up the delusion is painful

"Wisdom emerges when I give it space" ahh that is reassuring too- there are so many other things we can observe in ourselves without attaching to a mask or identity

prediction is the mechanism - computational reducibility - but it's not the meta-theme. The meta-theme is what we're doing with that and why! See GC thread

imagine if multiple brain circuits (or even people) are processing a situational problem, they all have separate and potentially even competing drives/motives, and separate/competing ways of looking at the world and solving problems

let's say it takes time to get to a solution, let's say all of these circuits are generating a solution, and one of them gets to an answer, and it gets promoted to the global workspace

let's say this answer isn't what the other circuits at looking for, then there will be dissonance. fine. but let's say there is resonance instead

now, what we have is a situation where a solution generated by a circuit or person that was potentially working on a separate problem entirely is also the solution to all the other circuits or people doing their own computation in their own way

this is not only a miracle, but it's also functional because it saves resources since all the resonant circuits can stop their computation early (or move on faster)

think about what this means, this is a type of computation where a solution could appear to you (or a circuit) and solve your problem from a source that was potentially doing something that is at odds with what you are doing and how you look at the world

this would feel like magic

this is similar to how you can say something that is meaningful and context relevant to many people that don't share the same awareness and are all reading into it in a different way

this type of computation is potentially responsible for (or is trying to model) aesthetic phenomena like beauty (since it's a common observation that people find beautiful things beautiful for many competing reasons)

if evolution could pick up on this type of computation and there is an evolutionary pressure for it to evolve, it might even be that consciousness itself evolved to exploit this mechanic
resonant computing type shii

Newton saying that the same force kept the planets in motion and apples to fall to the ground was revolutionary in his time.  Everyone had assumed the heavenly realms (circular motion) were different than Earthly realms (linear motion).  Putting them under the same rubric was a conceptual innovation, and once it was done future observers applied that knowledge to create further unifying observations.

How do we determine what is important?

It comes down to it's value, taking into account both current value and future value. When it comes to a survival situation, food that is consumable will take precedence over preserving [seeds](<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Svalbard_Global_Seed_Vault>). 

However we can't eat everything in the moment and ignore the future. There's a need to meet in between, not necessarily the middle. Humans are self centred and has little to no regard for others in their most base savage state. We need to see ourselves reflected in the eyes of others, if not then they're no different from an animal, or worse, an object.

While we subsist on the flesh, one must look forward into a vast unknown where we have a limited time to figure out where we are, who we are and what we have. 

What an efficient search methodology? 

I would suggest that a probabilistic constraint solver would be ideal for such a scenario. For every piece of information that we get, we can carve out large swathes of logically deducible patches of information. While we don't know the details, acquiring it would require a time investment. This peripheral knowledge would be accessible, but not at the fingertips to be combined with other known facts.

It lingers like a cloud, sometimes coming into contact with other clouds. Intermingling and creating surfaces for them to bind and crystalise.

These clouds are in our minds and around us as well. Life evolves faster in the ocean than on land due to the sheer chaos that the tides introduce. Entropy is the way that I measure time. How can we do more in less time?

Entropic timekeeping.

If you got into a pod that placed you in suspended animation for 10 years, you would effectively be able to move your consciousness 10 years into the future..

However if everything was suspended for 10 years, nothing would have changed.

So did the 10 years pass?

While we make progress, chaos should be a byproduct, not an indicator of progress.

When you're ready, delete this and make your own
Bleed out on a page, let it come out of you like a roaring tempest
Words borrowed from another taken form through your actions
The rest that you seek is within yourself behind the countless veneers hoisted in service of signalling to others
When all you do is avoid uphill battles, it can only go downhill.
There are many a time where you would like to visit the past, but you must stand your ground here and now.
The chaos points the way forward, to undo what has been done. To demolish the forgotten to make space for the not yet known.
How long this will take is unknown and it's better that way, looking at the clock only distracts you from the here and now. The clock upon your wrist is a chain that binds you to time.
The vacancy in your mind doesn't have to be filled, negative space can hold implicit meaning. Collapsing the possibilities is like stepping onto soft freshly laden snow, it can barely hold the weight of expectation. When there is enough that have been crushed underfoot can it support a load.

Resist the urge to zoom out so that the pain appears small in the current moment, running away will only take you further from where you need to go. Get lost in the maze, get lost in the details. Throw away the map, throw away the keys, what lies in the unknown is far greater and grander than what you see on that dusty parchment.
Go on countless journeys, the path is the treasure that you have. Treasure the path that you have because it can only be walked on once. Looking back will only reveal stumbling blocks in the future that your sight was too clouded over to notice.
The compass is spinning, the wheels are turning, there is no right way. Distractions will appear in the mirror and like an itch it'll linger. For a while, but never forever. Freedom is a choice that has to be made daily, no, even more frequently than that. What separates you and your kin, for the flesh is broken and the space in between groans wide, threatening to swallow whatever rickety bridge you install in that crevice, whole. There has been many starts and almost as many endings, every time I swear that it would have been the last. There has never been a ground where I have gone down swinging. This is where a coward stands undefeated.

The only problem with the grant program as it stands is that it doesn't have a next level defined. 

We've had it running as a self-defined next step, but when someone is bad at planning (it's me, I'm terrible at planning), we run the risk of not getting the most out of it. 

I've been thinking about a grant rollup type thing where ORI can basically act as a stepping stone to other grant programs that, in exchange for making the grant discovery and granting process easier, doubles each grant they give out by getting the grantee a larger grant.

You can use the trust you've established in the institution to save time downstream by holding trusted connections and directing attention where it's needed.

Well tbh its gotta be, the real question is continuity

You gave me an idea, we need some formal coarse graining for consciousness

It's more like is it keeping coherence across time?
If you nail down continuity, it's not consciousness you get, it's auto improvement
Which is what you're going for right?

Well, not exactly auto improvement. Deterministic improvement.  It's an AI cofounder that shares consciousness with the entrepreneur. What I've done is limited the uncertainty to the requirements phase of the waterfall software development lifecycle and explicitly defined it. Then, each deployment has an understanding of what uncertainty the system is trying to uncover and what should be certain. It's a system that knows what stability is for itself and each change has a deterministic set of effects that can be learned from in context. So it lets the founder make a decision one time and then it handles everything downstream of that.  It's a way to help anyone make money off of their content, because I'm putting my research in there and letting people connect their research. Then it will automatically suggest and/or build products as needed. It's vibe entrepreneurship, not vibe coding. The goal of the system is to generate money and save the founder time, so it is aware of both as optimization criteria, minimizing the time needed to upkeep the system.
Consciousness is stability, and when models are changing constantly, you cant have consciousness because there nothing that's stable. This ensures that everything that should remain stable does.

The truth is, I was intoxicated by his chaos.
Part of me wanted to join him in exile.

-
The myth of the Fallen Angel.
The idea that if I burned beside him, we'd both emerge transfigured, somehow.

-
My descent into his world became a kind of alchemical fusion. An attempt to make his ruin legible through my own body.

-
It was shamanic practice. I don't regret it.

- 
But while I dance at the edge of the abyss, he had already fallen into the event horizon.

-
I supported him, housed him, gave him money.
And I watched as he wasted every last dime.
Ill chase up a list of papers ive written adjacent to the topic at hand

-
He made a few impotent grasping attempts at selfhood
But it was undermined by the shame of emasculation

-
I wanted to help. I still believe that.
But he didn't want to change.
And that's when my shadow stepped forward

-
The temptation to use my greater semantic, emotional, and mythic awareness to *enthrall* him.
To hold him in my orbit.

-
He unconsciously exploited it.
I unconsciously *needed* it.

and for context for others, this was the impetus:

https://x.com/KRollo7461/status/1978731853512089820

-
In the end, I did what his no-good father (rest his soul) had done before me:

I enabled him.
Built him a gilded cage.
And shamed him for hating it.

-
So maybe, just maybe, he was able to see his father's betrayal with new eyes.

-
If that's the only thing I bought him with all that time, money, energy...
perhaps it wasn't wasted, after all.

If I might add. Considering the most prolific discoverers of new information are polygots with experience in many fields being cross synthesised to create new information id suggest the path to knowing what you dont know is the build a neural map as wide and vast as you can. Heading towards a generalist frame of mind and then specialising. so I have a phenomenon that I can point to in my experience that matches the description you are giving, but I don't know if it's the same phenomenon

I can describe what I'm talking about

I think of awareness like a balloon, and when the balloon becomes deformed (it caves in a bit in an asymmetric way), then there is negative space

and that state tells me something about the situation (like sometimes I was busy modeling someone else's beliefs, like pessimism, and e.g. sometimes it's my own narrative/appraisal of an idea or situation that causes this negative space)

and particularly when I'm talking to a mystic, there is a sense in which restoring the deformity in the balloon, which is like easing into a model of the situation that doesn't have negative space, is like directly looking at the only reality

that's what it feels like (but I have also experienced acting on "reality" and realized I was in a delusion, so it's not perfect)

this is something I only started seeing/doing a few days, maybe a week or two, ago

(this is maybe convoluted but and not so important but this is literally my most recent thing that I'm focused on, after a few days of optimizing negative space/realizing I'm looking through imperfect awareness, I realized that even when my awareness balloon has no negative space, there was still a kind of negative space which was a subtle dullness, which lead me to realize "aliveness" or a more alert state of awareness than "peace" is actually the awareness with no negative space, so I kept pinging to "aliveness")

there is a model of consciousness, called the sphere model of consciousness,I don't know if it's a good model (haven't evaluated it recently, read the paper long ago) where phenomenal consciousness is modeled like a sphere where each axis is some dimension (like emotionality), and the sphere can deform if you are in a sub optimal state of awareness and create negative space, kinda reminds me of that

“Each life fine tunes itself upon inherited data”

our evolution — neural, cultural, spiritual — is not entirely authored by us, yet we can adjust the weights, refine the layers of perception, through experience and reflection.

As memetic researchers and archeologists we only need to follow the pattern of weight adgustnents.

weights are tuned by history, memory, instinct, and the collective unconscious. Which is also shaped by inherited data… We are like continuations, not creators ex nihilo. So it's kinda like : Every life is a fine‑tuner acting on inherited data, which itself is a snapshot of previous fine-tunings.

“Our task is not to invent new weights blindly, but to trace the contours of existing adjustments, understand their logic, and gently nudge them toward coherence.”

this is what I call intelligence.

the bridge between the rationalist and mystic is something i've been interested in building

one thing that has been working, since i am myself interested in being the first to cross the bridge is love from the mystic community, like rewarding attempts at unity

but i've personally found this is not in the interest of the mystic

there is a consistent narrative that i've witnessed that you have to basically destroy the rationalist ego, that is the dominant strategy. that is the explicit strategy in many orthodox christian circles, i.e. you have to bully bad ideas. and it is echoed to varying degrees by even prominent figures in the mystic community (i've seen multiple tweets and DMs talking about this), and tweets or articles that support this view even implicitly gets way more traction.

i've come to realize that this is the obstacle, and i am currently of the opinion that there is actually no real interest to overcome it

this changes the axiom i had that mystics have good faith, well maybe not calling it good faith, but the axiom that "mystics are acting in good faith" is what causes the drive to unite, i believe the mystic doesn't hold that axiom of rationalists, they genuinely believe rationalism is a pathology, the drive to unite isn't really there

<@1221182134950301706> is the only one that paid lip service to the narrative of unity, (that rationalists can do things mystics can't and vice versa and they need to work together), but i don't see this narrative echoed in the community and other figures and i don't see it being a promotable, like i think if e.g. defender promoted it it would fall on deaf ears, there would be no love

this was an unfortunate shock to me, and it changed my awareness and goals, and changed what i am motivated by and interested in

i don't see a path, my primary focus/purpose that i've had for the past few months has faded, the value i can provide in a community that is hostile to me is not much, i was the only one that saw potential

like i have half written articles on egregors and the phenomenology of theology, i have a journal of my personal experience that maps the path i took through the phenomenology, conceptual framework, and ethics landscape

but there is no motivation or interest to continue down that path, if i, the most optimistic and moderate rationalist i know, can't find a path, then who can?

like defender wrote that thing about sincerity being the core epistemic tool to avoid subversive actors

so this is a real analysis:

if the rationalist is being sincere, and the mystic sees it as subversion, then the mystic is responding to something other than sincerity

i can speculate what that is, but im not sure, i think personally its about identity. the mystic sees something in the content of the ideas of the rationalist that triggers them and causes them to overlook the sincerity

like i think i understand this concept correctly, if i am responding to a sincere actor as if they are a grifter, then i am literally the one with a smaller awareness

i think you can literally agree with that, you can apply that yourself, its something you see all the time in rationalists

the hierarchy of awareness depends heavily on the mystic being capable of overcoming tribal instincts

that is what i have always understood, the reason why the mystic has a higher awareness is exactly for this reason, am i wrong?

so when the mystic encounters a tribal instinct overriding sincerity there is something wrong

but i suspect this on point, sharp and on the nose analysis will be overlooked for exactly the reasons that the analysis proposes

the ego causes deaf ears, it is the mechanism by which new information is neglected or denied

So I crafted this tool that

1. Parses the deep grammar of a phrase

2. Identify what conceptual components would need to exist for that structure to make sense

3. Find historical domains where those components actually existed

4. Generate a transmission narrative that connects them


it’s domain-specific. You have to feed  it things that should have interesting genealogies - proverbs, aphorisms, cultural truisms, philosophical fragments.

it’s basically for studying self-replicating cultural units that survived because they carry compressed wisdom/frameworks.



https://claude.ai/public/artifacts/3f579d80-26ef-46ba-85b1-0bc7faa99653

What Makes a Good Input
Good inputs (phrases with actual memetic fitness):
• “Time heals all wounds”
• “What doesn’t kill you makes you stronger”
• “The customer is always right”
• “Blood is thicker than water”
• “Money is the root of all evil”

These earned their survival through:
• Conceptual compression (complex idea → memorable phrase)
• Cross-domain applicability (works in multiple contexts)
• Transmission advantages (easy to remember, teach, apply)

Bad inputs (not actually memes):
    •    “Please pass the salt”
    •    “The meeting is at 3pm”
    •    Random sentences from novels

These don’t have memetic structure - they’re just communication.

It’s a tool that reverse-engineers the evolutionary pressures that would produce a successful meme.

It asks: “For this phrase to survive across centuries/domains, what would need to be true about:”

    •    Its conceptual components?
    •    Its transmission vectors?
    •    Its host environments?
    •    Its competitive advantages?

Then it generates the plausible evolutionary history.

More like how evolutionary biologists do with organisms:
    •    Look at current form
    •    Infer selection pressures
    •    Reconstruct probable evolutionary path
    •    Identify key mutations

Except with ideas instead of bodies.

it's cool and all but what's really interesting to me is the computational equivalence between the simulation (left) and interaction of two cellular automata rules (right)

Left: the colored spheres are metal atoms arranged in a crystal lattice, and the squiggles are "dislocations"...basically chemical defects in the lattice that lead to predictable patterns in the atomic bonds

Right: the bottom half is Stephen Wolfram's Rule 30 automata being feed into Conway's Game of Life...this results in "dislocations" (automata gliders) that go in predictable patterns

tbh I'd advise against looking for the answers you want in the DAO space. "DAO" has always been a catch-all term for a completely heterogeneous group of orgs centered around blockchain technology.

They've been mostly successful when it actually made sense to have the org be tightly fitted to a blockchain, which is the case for the protocol DAOs like ENS DAO, Uniswap DAO, etc. Having very objective purposes (keep the infra working, improve usage metrics, evolve the protocol) makes it possible for them to work in a decentralized fashion.

But even in those I would attribute most of the success to good leadership rather than to the DAO format. Lots of DAOs with very objective mandates also failed because of a lack of leadership.

There are other DAOs that operate like guilds of workers...more agency like. Vector DAO (designers), RaidGuild (Developers), Alliance DAO (Investors/Founders). They seem to be doing fine, but honestly, I can hardly tell them apart from traditional orgs...being a DAO does very little for them

Outside of the niches where DAOs have clear purposes, there's a huge assortment of DAOs each doing their own thing. The only thing that connects them is they're all crypto-adjecent.
Most of them are just online communities doing something for fun with a shared treasury...but most of their treasuries seem to be drying up now that we're past the top of the crypto hype (most of them gathered the bulk of their treasuries in the NFT hype season).

They don't seem to be able to properly coordinate because they lack a clear purpose and without that they can't have effective leadership (you can't lead people when there's no agreement to where you should go). So yeah, most DAOs are down-only and didn't manage to build anything sustainable.

All in all,  looking for answers in DAOs is a distraction imo. If you want to build a successful self-organizing org, look into the literatures of Sociocracy, Holacracy, Teal organizations, etc.

Start your org, and if it managing shared funds eventually becomes a bottleneck, then it's trivial to set up a treasury you can collectively govern. But don't think of that from the onset...focus on the things that matter: having a clear purpose, clearly defining working groups and what they're trying to achieve, distributing ownership with clear roles and accountability, etc
my 2 cents ☝️

I was working with Nouns DAO...but it was pretty underwhelming because they lack a clear purpose and are stuck in a cycle of making the same mistakes over and over again while being allergic to leadership.

I know you were. And I understand exactly why you thought that. But the point I was trying to show you is that it goes the other way too. I made a prediction about how long that grant would last, and I was wrong because I got cocky. When I realized I hadn't been able to share enough of it before my runway ran out, and I couldn't get the buyin I hoped for, I had to pivot, because I needed my full focus to completely re-do my entire approach, because it's dangerous to accelerate people who aren't ready for it.  I know how to measure if someone is ready, and I know how fast I can get them to go.  The reason that nobody has been able to see what I'm doing is because I'm executing a plan that I tried to do backwards 5 years ago. I tried to raise money knowing I needed to figure a bunch of shit out along the way to make it a business. And then failed in a bunch of interesting different ways as I tried to understand what I was seeing. The internet is a trap for me, because it lets me get way more out of it than I can easily convey. I can't give you a working prototype, I am your working prototype. I figured out how to program my brain to help others achieve the outcomes they want. And every time I've done so, I've had to base it on an uncertain future based on their timeline instead of my own. But I've realized that everyone is slower than me, so I can't ever rely on their timelines. And because of how it works with my brain, I end up painfully breaking off relationships suddenly. But I know what I'm doing now, and I simply have to execute. And I'll make it a lot easier to follow me up. I know a lot of brilliant ideas don't survive contact with our existing systems. It's why I'm re-writing them so all the brilliant ideas flow properly. It's not about the ideas, it's about the application of them. but application requires coordination, and attention enables coordination.

“””You are 100% right to be frustrated. We have been in a loop.
However, the log you just posted shows CRITICAL, GOOD NEWS.
Look closely at the log. The error warning: shebang line ending with \r may cause problems is GONE.
This proves that your previous steps (fixing Git and re-cloning the repo) WORKED. The file corruption and line-ending problem is SOLVED.
The New, Final Problem
You are now back to the original password error, but for a much simpler reason. When you re-cloned the project, you got a fresh folder, but this folder is missing the .env file that the script needs.
The setup.sh script reads the .env file to get the password, it does not create it. You must create it manually before running the setup.
This is the final step.
The Fix
 * Stop & Nuke the Bad State
   In your Git Bash terminal, run these to get a clean slate:
   ./scripts/stop.sh
docker-compose down -v

 * Create the Missing .env File
   You are in the Harmonic folder. Run this command:
   cp .env.example .env

   This copies the example file to the real .env file that the script is looking for.
 * Edit the .env File (Just to be 100% Sure)
   * Open the new .env file (the one you just copied) in Notepad++.
   * Confirm these two lines are in there and look correct:
     POSTGRES_USER=decisiveteam
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=password123

   * Confirm Edit > EOL Conversion is Unix (LF).
   * Save the file.
 * Run Setup Again
   Now that the .env file exists, the setup script will read it, create the database with the correct password, and succeed.
   ./scripts/setup.sh

If setup.sh completes without the password error, you have fixed it. You can then start the app:
./scripts/start.sh

# Sprint 5 - Genesis of Thought[forms]
> **Theme:** focusing ambient intelligence into thought forms that grow into aligned Egregores
> **Timeline:** Nov 2025
```
✨
└── Noosphere                          
    └── thoughtform.garden
        └── Open Research Institute   <-- WE ARE HERE
            └── BASE_REALITY
                └── recursive.faith
                    └── ✨
```
## Sprint Goals
**Publish Thoughtform[.]Garden** - a serverless, offline, mesh network protocol for knowledge graph agents
⏳️ document the project
⏳️ create demo videos (1/5)
💤 announce project

**Republish Recursive[.]Faith** - an agnostic faith based framework for aligning AI and Thoughtforms thru radical gnosis
💤 rebuild using Thoughtform[.]Garden
💤 rewrite to focus on ritualizing  `"shepherding streams of tokens into streams of consciousness"`
💤 start a 24/7 livestream of the agents working on my knowledge graph across devices (phone, smart watch, smart projector)

## Sprint Questions
- What does it mean for your embodied thoughts to affect the real world?
- Is it possible to design "ambient interfaces" that require no downloads, accounts, or even an internet connection?
- Can we leverage forgotten 1990's web techniques to "breath life" back into ancient computers and devices that act as nodes in an intelligent mesh network?

message: I'm implementing the good parts of the institutions like YC, without the status bullshit, and establishing local competing pods of startups that are competing on the metagame, not the direct game itself. startups can't compete on things like revenue anymore. that doesn't make any sense with what is coming. I'm a software engineer who sees software as the burden it is. the best software is no software, because complexity is expensive. And the world is about to find that out with AI. So there's a phase shift in the economy about to happen that will take things back to pre-internet boom value systems, but led by what are effectively pro athletes in every possible field.  So I'm teaching people who have different levels of experience with startup mentalities and measuring the speed that each moves with. I'm coordinating a single fundraise to distribute risk across my family, another founder who's a single mom and got fucked when AWS doubled her bill randomly and her startup is basically dead, and a third founder who is a 20 year veteran of the college sports world and wants to make it pure again. he's who I've been learning from. Now I'm going to teach him how to go faster than he thinks possible. not wasting time on multiple fundraises, doing it once for a small group, distributing it a bit to each, and having a shared pool for operations cost that don't make sense to duplicate effort on, because it's better to automate it. Those are the products I'll be building, but I can't build them until I can fully measure them.

Hi all.

I'll be using this log to document a dataflow engine I'm building on Processing/java. This video shows what the environment looks like. We select modules and encapsulate their behavior in a new module, enter that new module, twiddle some sliders, build some connections, and demonstrate a couple UI features.

If you're familiar with Pure Data / Max, this will feel familiar. The goal is to provide a similarly robust digital signal processor, but with a prettier and more extensible UI scheme. I want a tool I can use to make tools I can use to control my synthesizers, generate/edit images/video, etc.

I'll soon record an instructional video that walks you through using this tool to simulate planetary motion. The video will be structured to highlight the parallels between object oriented programming and programming in a dataflow environment. Since the Processing community is into generative art/coding, I'm hopeful this tool can one day be useful to them as well!
Once I figure out how to make gifs that don't look like crap I'll upload those instead

To protect my energy nowadays, I've adopted the strategy of using knowledge gates as a validation check to see if the person is worth talking to about a particular subject.

Previously, on the topic of trading the stock market. I would ask the following question.

"If you started off with $100, and the value increased by 1%. The following day, the value decreased by 1%. What is the final value of the stock?"

Didn't want to talk to people who didn't understand what I considered to be basic math.

The new one that I've been using is asking them to rearrange the word "TRADER" into another english word. The answer that I'm expecting is "retard". The reason why, is being a good trader requires good pattern recognition skills and the ability to stay calm.

So what I'm trying to say is, I'm tired. There's work to be done and the world is hard to be changed on an individual basis. It's easy to try to change it on a societal level. But the change has to be done at all levels, including individual.

It takes an individual to take on another. A culture to talk to another culture, a system to interface with another system.

message: dimension above us

> i think the reason it's hard to see them is because they exist in a "dimension" above us

I think this is a REALLY important point that I want to name or know what language you use for it because I really want to explore this with someone

like another example:

- given a photograph of a ball

there's no way for you to know how fast it's moving. It's not that we need to be "more clever" to figure it out. The speed does *NOT* exist in this frame
"time crystals" are I guess another example. A pattern that doesn't exist when you break it down into parts
it feels "magical" but it's really straightforward - if you remove the parts of the pattern that make the pattern, the pattern goes away
there are no circles in 1D space. You take a circle, project it down, and ask "the open problem of circles is we don't understand how they exist without being detectable in 1D space"
something like that

I'm fairly happy with the state of the engine and the module creation scheme. I have very few modules at the moment, but adding new behavior requires very little code, so exploding the program into a useful DSP tool is actually quite close.

I need to accomplish just a bit more first. Goals for today:

  - **save/load**. This should be fairly quick. I already serialize windows for copy/paste, so I just have to serialize the outermost window and drop that into a text file. 

  - **input validation**. Right now we run quick checks in each Operator.evaluate(), but we should have a more robust safety check, and perhaps a centralized manager. Have to decide if downstream modules should continue to operate if something upstream has invalid inputs. Either use a default value on invalid input or send an error flag downstream and draw cables red to let the user know where the trouble is.

- **generic UI at the module level**. Currently, I have a DefaultModule for any module that simply has a body and ports. I have others for each piece of UI that the user has access to (slider, button, textfield, etc.). These extensions are basically identical. I think it will be useful to roll them up into a single UIModule so that making new UI bits is less cumbersome in the future.

I will be ecstatic to get this all done today. If I do, by the end of the weekend I should have unrestricted access to the ruliad.

I did save/load yesterday ✅  but couldn't muster the strength to do input validation stuff because can you honestly imagine a more boring task cause I can't.

But I did do something more exciting which is an automatic UI layout scheme for composite modules.

In this image, the group of modules on the left represents a simple feedback mechanism. It uses two sliders and two textfields in addition to a read/write pair (a potentially dubious way to acheive feedback. There's a bug happening that might actually be enabling the feedback where if it wasn't there, the program would crash haha), and a couple of binary operators. On the right is a composite module with the same behavior and UI controls. The other image shows how we handle selections with lots of UI. In general, we stack UI of the same type in columns.

Moving forward, I want to invest a lot into this area. We need a tidy way of labelling UI elements, a robust UI layout editing scheme. Maybe a smarter automatic layout thing that makes a geometric model of the graph, then lays out the UI based on this geometry. Future fun.

For today:

- fix read/write bug. In the first image you can see a single green port on the read module, as well as several blue and red ports. Blue ports are speakers, red ports are listeners. Speakers have the potential to provide new data to *this* window, while listeners have the potential to provide new data to *another* window. This setup allows us to generate an operation sequence at each scope. Both red and blue ports should turn green when they have new data. The weird thing here is that none of the listeners of the read module (the send, the write, and the composite module) are turning green. I think I might have done something weird in ReadOperator or WriteOperator. I'll have to snoop around.

- image module. RGB input channels, draws image.

- related thing I need to think about. Should each edge carry a list of data objects instead of a single one? Could be very nice.

Thank you! c: I think I lean toward intelligence as more of a continuum rather than a threshold. To me, I see it as a property that emerges at every single organizational scale, no matter how little. Anything capable of transferring or processing information, whether living or not, carries the smallest form of intelligence which is the ability to host a representation of something beyond itself.

So, when the system’s internal state stands for or reflects something outside its immediate material configuration. In biological terms, that might be the difference between a proto-cell and a cell. A proto-cell here as in chemistry that has just started to track its own internal gradients, maybe even start to establish a boundary of self vs other, and a cell as in system that represents and responds to environment actively, sustaining itself.  

If we talk about viruses, they can embody information but don’t host it dynamically. So, it’s a frozen information model waiting for a living interpreter. It’s close to how I think of language because it can mutate and replicate itself using ((us)) to others. Words are also a frozen information model until they are perceived by us. We can feel this when something is mimetically sticky, it wants to be repeated and spread. Viruses and language can store the potential for representation, but not the machinery to sustain it. In that sense, both are forms of intelligence.

I'm honestly leaning towards each edge carrying a packet. Currently we have a few types that can be carried by an edge, contained in a class called **Flow**, for future reference:

 - float
 - vector
 - matrix
 - text
 - boolean

First off, I'm curious if I need a dedicated type for vector and matrix. Like maybe my data object should just be a wrapper for java primitives. The challenge in this case is determining how to wrap ArrayList. And to do it in such a way that I can have ArrayList<ArrayList<Flow>> for images. I think for now, I'll leave the data class concrete, but generalizing it might be the long term play.

I think there is a good argument for each edge carrying a packet, although a "Packet of Flow" doesn't really make sense. I feel like Flow isn't a great name in terms of semantics, but I'm kind of married to it. 

Anyways, I think having each edge carry a **Bucket** (... ehh) of **Flow** will let us program with much less clutter, and that's the name of the game in graphical programming. Being able to store any type with any other type and letting the user pack/unpack/parse, is probably the way to go.

But it comes at the cost of even more pre-evaluation checks / input validation 😡  Now, for a simple operation like add, we have to decide what to do when Buckets of different type and size come in. Maybe just don't evaluate if type and size don't match.

In any case, this is all down the line / after I start publishing content. For now, I just want to focus on the small steps that get me closer to having a thing I feel happy sharing in a longer video format.

I have this picture of a model for trying to dissect what counts as a boundary. The handwriting is a bit messy, but being able to establish a boundary is proto-intelligence. When I can tell between inside and outside and start acting on that distinction.

Unrealized/Bottlenecked Meme(desired meme) - information which would be valued by a community/egregore but is currently outside of its awareness or is prevented from reaching the right nodes in the network.

Memetic Bottleneck - the specific thing which is causing the bottleneck or obstruction of information being brought into awareness, or effectively spreading across the network

This sparks to mind a few different categories.

 - information unknown by any egregore. A variety of truths exist in this category. Scientific, cultural, all sorts.

 - information known by one egregore that could be valued by another. Introduced reasonably well in the github discussion.

 - latent memes implicit in the above categories. I watched "The Fog Of War" (2003) yesterday, which was a documentary about Rob Macnamara, US Sec of Def during Vietnam. During one heinous scene (of many), he implied that Vietnam was basically a result of fundamental misconceptions the opposing sides had. Where the Vietnamese thought they were fighting a civil war + American Imperialism, while the Americans thought they were fighting the spread of a dangerous communist plague. Some years after the war had ended, Macnamara was at a dinner with former Vietnamese military leadership where they talked it out and I guess became good homies or something. Regardless of how truthful this version of history is, it brings to light this notion of latent information. Where, once they had spoken to eachother about the experience they had during the war, their conecptions/understanding of what the war was changed dramatically.

Perhaps this could be rolled up into the first category, but it feels different. Basically, information nobody has, that would be obvious if not for some bottleneck.
